subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloud Sovereignty and Data Laws
description: A 60-second lesson on Cloud Sovereignty and Data Laws.

hook.text: When your data is 'in the cloud,' it might be stored on servers in another country entirely.

buildup.text: Europe's GDPR requires strict control over where personal data is stored and processed.

discovery.text: Cloud sovereignty means governments want local data centers under local legal jurisdiction.

twist.text: The U.S. CLOUD Act lets authorities access data on American company servers — anywhere worldwide.

climax.text: Nations are building sovereign clouds to keep sensitive data under their own laws.

punchline.text: Your data lives in the cloud. But whose laws apply?

quiz.question: What is 'cloud sovereignty'?
options: Governments requiring data to be stored under local legal jurisdiction
A cloud provider's right to delete any user's data
The ability of clouds to operate without electricity
